scene: 3 — A survey firm reads the open call and ticks the area it can staff
detail: The scene's captured AFTER-frame is the public call-to-apply detail page (still showing 'Respond to this Solicitation'), not the post-submission result. The action_trace genuinely ticked the checkbox and clicked a must_succeed Submit that succeeded, but the viewer lands on a page that reads as 'a call you could apply to' rather than 'a firm just applied'. The BEFORE-frame is the responses-management inbox, not the respond form with the checklist.
fix_recommendation: Re-script scene 3 so the AFTER-frame is the post-submit confirmation/submitted state (or the respond form mid-flow with the coverage checkbox visibly CHECKED + an active Submit), and use the actual respond form (checklist + empty selection) as the BEFORE-frame, so the before->after delta reads 'unticked -> ticked -> submitted'. This is a scene-scripting (spec actions) change, not a product change.

scene: 4 — Maya sees the firm tied to the coverage she designed
detail: The single response row renders the applicant org as 'Unknown Organization' and SUBMITTED BY is the reviewer's own account (jonathan / jjackson@dimagi.com). For a scene whose job is tying a REAL FIRM to a coverage area, an anonymous firm submitted by the author's email undercuts the 'real firm vs real survey' narrative. (Stems from the deliberate decision to use the real logged-in session as the applicant — which is honest, but reads thin on the responses list.)
fix_recommendation: Either (a) accept the real-session identity but have the product render a clear fallback display name when org_name is empty (e.g. the contact's name or 'Your organization') rather than the bare 'Unknown Organization' string; or (b) seed a named respondent org/persona for the demo. Needs a user pick between authenticity (a) and a scripted firm (b).

scene: 2 — Maya mints the call live and lands on its responses page
detail: On the freshly-minted call's responses landing, the load-bearing 'Active' status and the new solicitation id are under-surfaced (id only in the cropped URL) while a bare 'No responses yet' empty state dominates the body — softening the 'I just minted a live call' beat.
fix_recommendation: Add a one-time create-success affordance on landing ('Solicitation created — now Active') + render the id as a chip near the title, and raise the 'Active' status's visual weight above the empty inbox.

scene: 1 — Maya turns her finished study into a call for a firm
detail: The R6 study card under-presents its load-bearing facts: the intervention/comparison composition and the '2 wards' count are buried as throwaway secondary text, three near-identical greens do overlapping emphasis work, and ~55% of the viewport is empty.
fix_recommendation: Render 'Intervention: Attakar · Comparison: Gura · 2 wards' as a legible key-value block on the card, collapse the redundant readiness signaling to one canonical state chip, and optionally surface the first row of in-progress plans so the portfolio reads as a portfolio.

scene: 3 — A survey firm reads the open call and ticks the area it can staff
detail: ADVISORY (non-blocking). Narration claims the firm ticks the area and submits, but the captured after-frame is the pre-application public page with no submitted/confirmation state, and 'submission blocked until one chosen' is shown nowhere. Two load-bearing claims rest on the trace, not the frame.
fix_recommendation: Same as the scene-3 concept_clarity finding — landing the after-frame on the submitted state resolves this too. Advisory; logged for later triage.
